formal: Good morning
How do you do?
Pleased to meet you
wrong: How do you do? (to a friend at a party)
correct: Hey! What's up? (casual setting)
explanation: Usa saludos informales con amigos, formales en trabajo/negocios

formal: It appears we're having quite pleasant weather today
The forecast suggests mild temperatures
wrong: It appears we're having quite pleasant weather today (to a coworker at the office)
correct: Nice day today! (casual conversation)
explanation: El clima es un tema seguro para charla casual. Usa lenguaje relajado con conocidos

spanish: Charla casual: planes de fin de semana
formal: What are your plans for the weekend?
How do you intend to spend your leisure time?
informal: Any fun plans for the weekend?
wrong: How do you intend to spend your leisure time? (to a friend)
correct: What you doing this weekend? (casual with friends)
explanation: Los planes de fin de semana son tema seguro en conversaciones casuals

formal: What field of work are you in?
How do you find your professional endeavors?
informal: What do you do for work?
wrong: How do you find your professional endeavors? (to someone at a party)
correct: What do you do for work? (casual conversation)
explanation: El trabajo es un tema común. Mantén profesional pero casual dependiendo del contexto

formal: I would like to invite you to dinner on Saturday
Would you care to join us for coffee?
informal: You should come to my party!
wrong: Would you care to join us for coffee? (inviting your best friend)
correct: Wanna grab coffee sometime? (casual with friends)
explanation: Las invitaciones formales son para eventos profesionales, las informales para amigos

formal: Thank you very much for the invitation. I would be delighted to attend.
I appreciate the offer and shall confirm my attendance.
wrong: Yeah, I'd love to! (accepting a formal business dinner invitation)
correct: Thank you very much for the invitation. I would be delighted to attend. (formal event)
explanation: Responde con entusiasmo casual con amigos, formalmente en eventos de negocios

formal: I appreciate the invitation, but I'm afraid I have a prior commitment
Thank you for inviting me. Unfortunately, I won't be able to attend.
informal: Thanks for the invite, but I can't make it
I'd love to, but I'm busy that day
wrong: No, I don't want to go. (declining an invitation)
correct: I'd love to, but I'm busy that day. Rain check? (polite decline)
explanation: Siempre rechaza invitaciones cortésmente dando una razón, incluso si es falsa

formal: That's an excellent presentation
You have demonstrated remarkable skill
wrong: You're pretty. (to someone in a professional setting)
correct: That's great work on this project! (professional compliment)
explanation: Los cumplidos deben ser apropiados para el contexto y situación

formal: Thank you, that's very kind of you to say
I appreciate your feedback
wrong: Yeah, I know. (responding to a compliment arrogantly)
correct: Thank you! That's very kind of you to say. (gracefully receiving)
explanation: Acepta cumplidos con gracia. Nunca digas 'lo sé' o niegues el cumplido

formal: I sincerely apologize for my behavior
Please accept my apologies for the inconvenience
wrong: Sorry about that! (apologizing to your boss for a major mistake)
correct: I sincerely apologize for my error. Let me correct it immediately. (formal apology)
explanation: Apólogos formales para errores graves, informales para cosas menores

formal: I cannot thank you enough
I'm deeply grateful for your assistance
Your support has been invaluable
wrong: Okay. (responding to someone who helped you)
correct: Thank you so much! I really appreciate your help. (expressing gratitude)
explanation: Agradece siempre a quien te ayuda, especialmente en contextos importantes

formal: Congratulations on your promotion
I wish to extend my congratulations on this achievement
wrong: Nice job. (to someone who just got promoted)
correct: Congratulations on your promotion! That's incredible! (enthusiastic)
explanation: Felicita sinceramente con entusiasmo apropiado para la ocasión

formal: I extend my deepest condolences to you and your family
Please know that my thoughts are with you during this difficult time
informal: I'm so sorry for your loss
I'm here for you if you need anything
wrong: That's sad. (to someone whose family member passed away)
correct: I'm so sorry for your loss. Please let me know if there's anything I can do. (sincere)
explanation: Expresa simpatía genuina y ofrece ayuda concreta cuando alguien está pasando por algo difícil

formal: Would you be able to assist me with this matter?
Could I possibly impose on you to help me with...?
informal: Could you give me a hand?
wrong: Help me. (demanding help)
correct: Could you help me with this? I'd really appreciate it. (politely asking)
explanation: Pide ayuda cortésmente usando 'could you' o 'would you mind'

formal: May I be of assistance?
Would you like some help with that?
informal: I can help if you'd like
Let me give you a hand
wrong: (Doing nothing and watching someone struggle)
correct: Would you like some help with that? I'd be happy to assist. (offering help)
explanation: Ofrece ayuda activamente cuando veas a alguien luchando con algo

formal: It was a pleasure to meet you
I look forward to our next meeting
Farewell
wrong: Bye! (leaving a formal business meeting)
correct: It was a pleasure to meet you. I look forward to our next meeting. (formal goodbye)
explanation: Usa despedidas formales en contextos profesionales, informales con amigos

formal: Good morning. This is [name] calling from [company]
How may I assist you today?
wrong: Hey! What's up? (professional business call)
correct: Good morning. This is Sarah calling from ABC Company. (professional phone call)
explanation: Las llamadas profesionales requieren introducciones formales y claras

formal: Please let me know your availability
Thank you for the update
wrong: hey mate, u free? (professional text to boss)
correct: Hi, are you available for a meeting tomorrow? (professional text)
explanation: Mantén un lenguaje más formal en textos profesionales, casual con amigos

wrong: Using formal language in a casual Instagram post
correct: That outfit is 🔥 (casual social media)
explanation: Las redes sociales son espacios informales. Usa lenguaje relajado y emojis

informal: Do you want to go out sometime?
I have a crush on you
wrong: I find your genetic material suitable for reproduction. (on a first date)
correct: I really like you. Would you like to go out sometime? (asking someone out)
explanation: Las frases de citas son informales pero respetuosas. Sé directo pero amable

formal: I'd like to introduce you to...
May I present...
wrong: (Leaving someone standing alone without introducing them)
correct: Hey, I want you to meet my friend John. John, this is Sarah. (polite introduction)
explanation: Presenta a las personas claramente con nombres completos y relación

formal: I propose we meet on Friday at 2 PM
Would next Tuesday suit you for a meeting?
wrong: Friday. 2 PM. (making plans without negotiation)
correct: Would Friday work for you? We could meet at 2 PM. (suggesting and confirming)
explanation: Sugiere tiempo y lugar, confirma la otra persona está de acuerdo

formal: I regret to inform you that I must cancel our appointment
Unfortunately, I am unable to attend due to unforeseen circumstances
informal: I can't make it tomorrow, sorry
I have to bail on our plans
wrong: (Not showing up and not telling anyone)
correct: I'm so sorry, but something came up. Can we reschedule? (canceling respectfully)
explanation: Cancela con anticipación, da razón, y propón otra fecha

formal: In my opinion...
I believe that...
It appears to me that...
wrong: Your idea is stupid. (dismissing someone's idea)
correct: I see your point, but I think we could try a different approach. (respectful disagreement)
explanation: Expresa opiniones de forma respetuosa. Usa frases como 'I think' or 'In my opinion'

formal: I concur with your assessment
I must respectfully disagree
informal: I see what you mean, but...
wrong: You're wrong. (dismissing someone)
correct: I understand your point, but I look at it differently. (respectful disagreement)
explanation: Respeta opiniones diferentes. Usa frases como 'I see what you mean, but...'

formal: Moving on to another matter...
If I may change the subject...
wrong: (Abruptly interrupting without acknowledging the topic)
correct: That's interesting. Speaking of which, did you hear about...? (smooth topic change)
explanation: Cambia de tema suavemente usando frases de transición naturales

formal: It has been a pleasure speaking with you
I must attend to other matters
Thank you for this conversation
informal: It was great chatting with you!
wrong: (Stopping mid-conversation and walking away)
correct: I've really enjoyed talking with you, but I should get going. Let's stay in touch! (graceful exit)
explanation: Termina conversaciones elegantemente expresando apreciación y despidiéndote claramente

formal: I entirely agree with your assessment
That is precisely my sentiment
wrong: Yeah. (unenthusiastic agreement)
correct: Absolutely! I completely agree with you! (enthusiastic agreement)
explanation: Cuando estés de acuerdo, muestra entusiasmo usando palabras fuertes

formal: I must respectfully beg to differ
While I appreciate your perspective, I hold a different view
informal: I see where you're coming from, but...
That's not really how I see it
wrong: That's wrong. (harsh disagreement)
correct: I appreciate your point, but I see it differently. Here's what I think... (tactful disagreement)
explanation: Discrepa educadamente validando la otra persona primero

spanish: Manejar la incomodidad en charla casual
formal: How interesting
I see
That's quite remarkable
wrong: (Staring in silence)
correct: Oh interesting! Tell me more about that. (engaging in small talk)
explanation: Si hay silencio incómodo, haz preguntas de seguimiento para mantener la conversación
